preview_text:
The model favors Algeria with a 45% win probability, but the match remains competitive. Jordan holds a 25% chance of victory, meaningfully above the sportsbook consensus of 15%, suggesting the model rates the underdog's prospects considerably higher than the market does.

The draw is a material outcome at 30%, indicating the teams are expected to be reasonably evenly matched in open play. The three-way spread—with Algeria ahead but neither team dominant—points to a match where contingency and execution matter alongside underlying strength. A Jordan upset or stalemate both carry meaningful probability mass.

Model and market diverge notably on Jordan's chances, a gap of roughly 10 percentage points. The sportsbook and Polymarket are closely aligned with each other, both pricing Algeria near 62–65%. The dashboard makes no claim about which assessment is correct; the difference likely reflects either model and market weighting available information differently or market inefficiency. The gap warrants attention from those backing either side.
